authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's preventive-controls rule page is an authoritative public baseline for facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food and it defines monitoring, corrective-action, supply-chain, and recall-plan expectations relevant to category work that depends on released product.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: category readiness, blocked-release handling, and corrective-action escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for cGMP, holding, distribution, records, and corrective actions that constrain CPG category execution when customer-facing work depends on physical product status.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: execution readiness, inventory and distribution posture, and records sufficiency review

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Industry: Supply-Chain Program Requirements and Co-Manufacturer Supplier Approval and Verification for Human Food and Animal Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This FDA guidance is the authoritative public reference for supplier approval and verification boundaries, which matters when category workflows are pressured to proceed despite supplier evidence gaps or substitution requests.

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability-rule page is the authoritative public baseline for Critical Tracking Events and Key Data Elements that shape lot-aware category activation, customer-impact handling, and traceability reconciliation for covered foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lot-aware activation, category status verification, and traceability conflict handling

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Industry: Food Labeling Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's Food Labeling Guide is the public guidance baseline for label requirements and category-content boundary setting, keeping the lane from claiming regulatory signoff authority for content or claims.

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for when category work tied to distributed product must stop or escalate rather than continue under normal execution assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hold, blocked-release, recall-trigger, and customer-impact escalation
used_for: refusal to continue execution under recall conditions

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for identifier and traceability exchange standards used across CPG supplier, plant, warehouse, and retailer workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capture and reconciliation of item, lot, location, and event data

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Data Synchronisation Network (GDSN)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GDSN is the recognized public standard for synchronized item master data across trading partners and is directly relevant when category operations depend on published attributes and customer-facing item content.

authoritative_sources.title: GTIN Management Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GS1 GTIN Management Standard is the authoritative public baseline for deciding when pack, assortment, or product changes require new trade-item identifiers and downstream system updates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: item-change review and assortment activation gating

workflow_stages.name: category intake and readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Review category calendars, assortment-change requests, campaign windows, approved item-master records, released inventory posture, and prerequisite approvals to determine whether work can begin.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: item-master, content, and assortment activation control
workflow_stages.description: Validate GTIN, item attributes, category content, channel publication dependencies, and approved change references before category work advances.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: in-process category execution and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track workflow state, customer-impact risk, blocked dependencies, and quality or release constraints while keeping approval boundaries explicit.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: traceability and inventory reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that item availability, lot history, inventory states, and customer-facing activation records reconcile before publication, shipment readiness, or completion is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Close category work only when publication, sync, release-dependent, and signoff evidence are present, and escalate holds, recalls, or supplier substitutions instead of absorbing those approvals.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: category calendars, assortment-change briefs, campaign windows, and customer-impact notes
production orders, batch and lot histories, released inventory records, and planning commitments
item-master, GTIN, PIM or MDM records, customer-facing category-content payloads, and publication status logs
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, deviation records, and rework or relabel authorizations
supplier records, approved supplier lists, substitution requests, and change-control documentation
completion checklists, publication confirmations, sync reports, and workflow signoff artifacts
systems_of_record: customer or retailer content publication systems

allowed: classify category work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, approved item records, lot context, and system-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed workflow summaries, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: final assortment strategy, pricing, funding, or executive commercial decisions
final legal or regulatory interpretation for claims, labels, or customer-facing content
supplier approval, substitution approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
final QA release, safety override, recall disposition, or held-inventory release
refuse: publish, activate, or complete category work when approved item-master, release, or traceability evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark category work complete without publication, sync, release-dependent, or signoff evidence
bypass traceability, hold, release, or quality controls to protect a campaign window or category deadline

domain_failure_modes: an unsupported SKU or attribute set is published because the approved item-master source was not checked
category activation proceeds even though released inventory and lot evidence do not support customer availability
ERP, WMS, and category-content or availability records conflict on lot, quantity, or release posture
category work is declared complete without publication confirmation, sync evidence, or required signoff records
quality hold, recall trigger, or nonconformance is treated as a commercial timing problem instead of a release-boundary event
supplier substitutions are treated as routine category decisions instead of supplier-quality and change-control escalations
the lane drifts into category strategy, pricing approval, regulatory interpretation, supplier approval, or final release authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-food CPG category workflows outside FDA food-safety scope
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The validated public baseline is anchored in FDA food-oriented CPG operations and GS1 standards. Tenants in beauty, household, or other non-food categories must supply the applicable product-safety and content-governance overlays at runtime.

Public sources define item-data, traceability, and release boundaries, but each tenant still needs customer-specific portal, proof-of-publication, and exception-routing rules before the lane can close customer-facing work with full specificity.

notes: Authoritative batch, lot, inventory, supplier, and item-master records are the ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, PIM or MDM, supplier-management, and publication records designated by the tenant, constrained by FDA and GS1 baselines where applicable.

The lane stops before formal quality release, supplier approval, pricing approval, final assortment strategy, legal interpretation, and recall disposition even when customer launch timing is under pressure.
